title: Reading and Use of English – Part 2
description: For questions 9–16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only ONE word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Minimalism is a lifestyle choice centered (0) around the idea that owning fewer possessions can lead to a more meaningful life. (9) Although it has recently gained popularity through social media, the core principles of minimalism have existed for centuries in various cultures. (10) Such is the pressure of modern consumerism that many people feel overwhelmed by the constant urge to buy new things. (11) Provided that an individual focuses on what truly adds value to their life, the process of decluttering can be incredibly liberating. (12) Not only does minimalism reduce financial stress, but it also allows for greater mental clarity and focus. (13) Regardless of the size of one’s home, the practice of intentional living can be applied to any environment. (14) Not until people start letting go of physical items do they realize how much emotional weight they were carrying. (15) Despite the initial difficulty of breaking old habits, many find that a simpler life is far more rewarding than they expected. (16) Whether minimalism is a passing trend or a permanent shift in societal values is still being discussed by sociologists. This movement encourages us to prioritize experiences over objects and find contentment in the present moment.

example.text: Minimalism is a lifestyle choice centered (0) around the idea that owning fewer possessions.
example.explanation: Preposition used to indicate the central theme or focus.

questions.explanation: Conjunction introducing a contrast or concession.
Determiner used for emphasis at the start of the clause.
Part of the 'Not only... but also' structure.
Part of the fixed linker 'Regardless of'.
Part of the 'Not until' structure triggering inversion ('do they realize').
Preposition used to show contrast or concession.
Conjunction introducing two possibilities or a doubt.
